findings.evidence: `Basic.lean:212` has `{E ...} [NormedSpace ℝ E] {g : ℝ → E}`; Mathlib defines `iteratedDerivWithin (n : ℕ) (f : 𝕜 → F)` in `Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/IteratedDeriv/Defs.lean:53` and the used `ContDiffOn.differentiableOn_iteratedDerivWithin` is also over `𝕜` at `Defs.lean:162`.
findings.fix: State it over `{𝕜 : Type*} [NontriviallyNormedField 𝕜] {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace 𝕜 E] {g : 𝕜 → E} {s : Set 𝕜}`, with `ContDiffOn 𝕜 ...`, `UniqueDiffOn 𝕜 s`, and `x : 𝕜`; keep the CM wrapper as the real specialization.
findings.issue: `ContDiffOn.hasDerivAt_iteratedDerivWithin` is in the generic `ContDiffOn` namespace but fixes the domain/scalars to `ℝ`.

summary: One exported generic calculus lemma is still below the natural Mathlib level: it is stated only over `ℝ` although the iterated-derivative API it wraps is field-generic.
